_template.name: FluxNova: Post-Trade Settlement
_template.description: Post-trade settlement blueprint with counterparty gateway, clearing house connector, regulatory reporting, and settlement database built on FluxNova BPM platform
_template.author: CalmStudio Contributors

nodes.name: FluxNova Platform
nodes.description: Full FluxNova BPM platform deployment hosting the post-trade settlement process

nodes.name: FluxNova BPM Engine
nodes.description: Core BPMN 2.0 / DMN 1.3 engine orchestrating trade confirmation, netting, novation, and settlement lifecycle workflows

audit-logging.description: All settlement process events, trade state transitions, and regulatory submissions are recorded in an immutable audit log

nodes.name: FluxNova REST API
nodes.description: RESTful API layer for trade submission, settlement status queries, and external task worker integration

nodes.name: FluxNova Cockpit
nodes.description: Process monitoring dashboard providing real-time visibility into settlement process instances, failed trades, and regulatory deadlines

nodes.name: FluxNova Admin
nodes.description: Management console for counterparty onboarding, user administration, and settlement platform configuration

nodes.name: FluxNova Tasklist
nodes.description: Task management UI for trade exception handling, manual matching, and compliance review tasks in the settlement workflow

nodes.name: Process Database
nodes.description: Relational database storing settlement process definitions, runtime state, trade history, and compliance audit logs

nodes.name: Counterparty Gateway
nodes.description: Secure gateway for counterparty trade confirmations and matching via FIX, FpML, and SWIFT message protocols

encryption-in-transit.description: All counterparty communications use mTLS to authenticate both parties and encrypt trade confirmation data

audit-logging.description: All inbound and outbound counterparty messages are logged with timestamps for regulatory audit trails

nodes.name: Clearing House Connector
nodes.description: Connector to central clearing house (CCP) for trade novation, netting, and multilateral settlement instruction submission

encryption-in-transit.description: Clearing house connectivity uses leased line or dedicated VPN with TLS for trade submission integrity

nodes.name: Regulatory Reporting Service
nodes.description: Automated regulatory reporting service generating EMIR, MiFIR, and Dodd-Frank trade reports with real-time submission to trade repositories

regulatory-compliance.description: All trade reports are validated against ESMA and CFTC schemas before submission; submission receipts are archived for 7 years

nodes.name: Settlement Database
nodes.description: Settlement positions, obligations, and trade lifecycle data store — source of truth for net settlement positions and regulatory reporting

encryption-in-transit.description: All connections to the settlement database use TLS-encrypted JDBC

relationships.description: Engine persists settlement process state, history, and audit data to the process database
REST API delegates all requests to the embedded engine via internal Java API calls
Cockpit queries settlement process instances and compliance deadlines via the REST API
Admin manages counterparty onboarding, users, and platform configuration via the REST API
Tasklist retrieves and completes trade exception and compliance review tasks via the REST API
FluxNova platform contains the BPM engine
FluxNova platform contains the REST API
FluxNova platform contains the Cockpit monitoring app
FluxNova platform contains the Admin management app
FluxNova platform contains the Tasklist app
FluxNova platform contains the process database
Engine invokes the counterparty gateway for trade confirmation matching and settlement instruction exchange
Engine submits cleared trades for novation and netting via the clearing house connector
Engine triggers regulatory report generation after trade confirmation and settlement instruction creation
Engine reads and writes settlement positions and obligations to the settlement database during workflow execution
Regulatory reporting service reads consolidated settlement positions from the settlement database for report generation
